system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices

    (20  20  1)       // 6

);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air

        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {

        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );

    frontAndBack
    {

            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Ekp)    Gauss upwind;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

{
    solver          ode;
    method          standard;
}

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs
{
    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 36.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This is the same error from Attempt 2, indicating that the `species` keyword is still not found at the top level of `physicalProperties`. Looking at the history of attempts, various approaches have been tried:

1. Switching from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture` ✓ (fixed)
2. Adding a top-level `species` dictionary with flat format ❌ (wrong format)
3. Adding a top-level `species` dictionary with sub-dict format ❌ (still wrong location)
4. Moving `species` inside `multiComponentMixture` ❌ (didn't take effect)

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file again, I see the issue. The `multiComponentMixture` block exists but it references `"specie"` while the `thermoType` specifies `specie` as a separate entry. More importantly, the `species` list is defined **outside** the `multiComponentMixture` block, and the `multiComponentMixture` itself doesn't contain a `species` sub-dictionary.

In OpenFOAM 10 with `multiComponentMixture`, the `species` information must be available either at the top level OR within the `multiComponentMixture` block. Since the current file has `multiComponentMixture` without a `species` entry inside it, and there's a separate top-level `species` that may not be recognized by the mixture model, the lookup fails.

## Root Cause

The `multiComponentMixture` block in `physicalProperties` does not contain a `species` sub-dictionary. When `multiComponentMixture` is used, OpenFOAM looks for the species list either at the top level of the thermophysical properties dictionary or within the `multiComponentMixture` block itself. The current file has a separate top-level `species` that may not be picked up correctly by the mixture constructor.

## Proposed Fix

### 1. Modify `constant/physicalProperties` — Restructure `multiComponentMixture`

Inside the `multiComponentMixture` block, add a `species` sub-dictionary with the proper `{ value ...; }` format, and remove the separate top-level `species` entry. The `multiComponentMixture` should look like:

```
multiComponentMixture
{
    specie        specie;
    species       (
                    CH4       { value 16.043; };
                    O2        { value 31.999; };
                    N2        { value 28.014; };
                    H2O       { value 18.015; };
                    CO2       { value 44.01; };
                  );
    multiComponentAssemblies
    {
        assem1
        {
            reactions   ("CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O");
            specie      ("CH4" "O2" "CO2" "H2O");
        }
    }
}
```

And remove the standalone `species` dictionary from the top level of the file entirely. The `specie` (singular) entry in `thermoType` should remain as-is.

### 2. Verify `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

Ensure all five species (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2) have complete `janaf` thermodynamic blocks with `Cp`, `H`, `S`, and `T` coefficients. Missing species will cause errors after this fix resolves the current one.

### Summary

The key fix is embedding the `species` list **inside** the `multiComponentMixture` block with proper dictionary-format entries (`{ value ...; }`), replacing the separate top-level `species` entry. This ensures the `multiComponentMixture` constructor can find the species information it needs.</review_analysis>

Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
